description: Literary text in Arabic script. Includes the well-known story of Ibn al-Sammāk who visited Hārūn al-Rashīd, who was drinking water. Ibn al-Sammāk stopped him from drinking and asked what he would give to be able to drink if he were forbidden from doing so. "My whole kingdom!" He then let him drink, upon which he asked what he would give to be able to urinate it out. "My whole kingdom!" Ergo, a kingdom is worth nothing more than a drink and some urine.
description_he: טקסט ספרותי בכתב ערבי; כולל סיפורו המפורסם של אבן אל-סמאק שביקר את הארון אל-רשיד; הלקח: ממלכה שווה רק שתייה ושתן
